text: Artificial intelligence is becoming a powerful part of our daily lives, but using it responsibly raises important ethical questions. One key concern is bias: if an AI is trained on data that contains old, unfair decisions, it may repeat them. This can lead to unfair treatment of certain groups, so fairness must be carefully built into systems. Privacy is another major issue, as many AI tools collect personal data. People need to know what is being collected and why. This is where transparency comes in—companies should explain how their AI works and makes decisions. Ultimately, responsible AI development means creating technology that respects human rights and avoids causing harm.
